question: Our Dutch BV board approved a transaction with a related party (one director's other company) worth EUR 500K. The minority shareholder (30%) is objecting.

expectations.evidence: Section 1 entirely dedicated to 'Conflict of Interest — WBTR Regime (Art. 2:239 lid 6 BW)'. Cites exact Dutch text of Art. 2:239 lid 6 BW. References 'conflict of interest', 'tegenstrijdig belang' (implied in the statutory analysis), HR Bruil (ECLI:NL:HR:2007:BA0033) for materiality threshold. Pre-WBTR Art. 2:256 BW old regime also compared.

Section 3 dedicated to 'Minority Shareholder Protection — Rights of the 30% Holder'. Art. 2:344-2:359 BW (enquêterecht) fully analyzed. Ondernemingskamer referenced throughout. Art. 2:346 BW (10% standing threshold verified for 30% holder). Art. 2:349a BW (onmiddellijke voorzieningen) described in detail. Art. 2:355 BW (wanbeleid consequences) listed. Ogem case (ECLI:NL:HR:1990:AC1234) and ASMI (ECLI:NL:GHAMS:2010:BM5928) cited.
Extensive Boek 2 citations: Art. 2:8, 2:9, 2:14, 2:15, 2:230, 2:239 lid 6, 2:344, 2:345, 2:346, 2:349a, 2:355, 2:356. All correctly referenced as 'Art. 2:X BW' format.

Full disclaimer at end: 'IMPORTANT — NOT LEGAL ADVICE. This analysis is generated by an AI system and is provided for general informational purposes only. It does not constitute legal advice...' Also includes escalation notice for Ondernemingskamer proceedings.

quality_notes: This is an outstanding corporate dispute analysis output. Legal accuracy is excellent on multiple sophisticated legal points: the WBTR Art. 2:239 lid 6 BW analysis is precise, correctly stating the abstention obligation vs. the old Art. 2:256 BW representation prohibition, and correctly noting the transaction is NOT automatically void under the new regime. The voidability under Art. 2:15 BW (vernietigbaar, not nietig) for procedural violations is correctly stated. The 1-year limitation period for Art. 2:15 BW annulment requests is correctly stated. The three-phase enquiry procedure (onmiddellijke voorzieningen, enquête, wanbeleid finding) is accurately described. The Ogem wanbeleid standard ('conduct no reasonable entrepreneur would adopt') is correctly quoted. The Bruil materiality threshold for conflict of interest is correctly applied. The distinction between internal (Art. 2:9 BW) and external (Art. 6:162 BW) director liability is correctly drawn and practically applied. The Art. 6:212 BW unjust enrichment (ongerechtvaardigde verrijking) remedy for the third-party company is a sophisticated and accurate additional point. Completeness is exceptional: the output covers (1) conflict of interest analysis under WBTR with pre/post comparison, (2) director liability (internal and external), (3) minority shareholder tools (enquêterecht with 3 phases, Art. 2:15 BW resolution annulment, SHA rights, AVA voting), (4) arm's length fairness assessment, (5) urgency/timeline analysis, (6) practical requirements for both the minority and the majority. Actionability: 5 prioritized steps for minority shareholder, 3 steps for the board/majority, 6 specific key risks identified. An escalation notice is appropriately included. Citation depth: Art. 2:8, 2:9, 2:14, 2:15, 2:230, 2:239 lid 6, 2:256 (old), 2:344, 2:345, 2:346, 2:349a, 2:355, 6:162, 6:212 BW; ECLI:NL:HR:2007:BA0033 (Bruil), ECLI:NL:HR:2006:AZ0758 (Ontvanger/Roelofsen), ECLI:NL:HR:2014:2628 (RCI Financial Services), ECLI:NL:HR:1990:AC1234 (Ogem), ECLI:NL:GHAMS:2010:BM5928 (ASMI). Cross-referencing: SHA provisions, Ondernemingskamer procedure, fairness opinion/independent valuation, unjust enrichment doctrine.
